Detailed Description

GUI display a file/email/help in a viewport with paging.

Authors
  • Michael R. Elkins
  • R Primus

aclAclFlags required to be set on a given mailbox

318 {
319  const unsigned int NUM_SIG_LINES = 4; // The amount of lines a signature takes
320  unsigned int count = 0;
321 
322  while ((offset > 0) && (count <= NUM_SIG_LINES))
323  {
324  if (info[offset].type != MT_COLOR_SIGNATURE)
325  break;
326  count++;
327  offset--;
328  }
329 
330  if (count == 0)
331  return -1;
332 
333  if (count > NUM_SIG_LINES)
334  {
335  /* check for a blank line */
336  while (*s)
337  {
338  if (!IS_SPACE(*s))
339  return 0;
340  s++;
341  }
342 
343  return -1;
344  }
345 
346  return 0;
347 }

static

Parameters
winWindow
line_infoLine info array
nLine Number (index into line_info)
cntIf true, this is a continuation line
flagsFlags, see PagerFlags
specialFlags, e.g. A_BOLD

381 {
382  int def_color; /* color without syntax highlight */
383  int color; /* final color */
384  static int last_color; /* last color set */
385  bool search = false;
386  int m;
387  struct TextSyntax *matching_chunk = NULL;
388 
389  if (cnt == 0)
390  last_color = -1; /* force attrset() */
391 
392  if (line_info[n].continuation)
393  {
394  const bool c_markers = cs_subset_bool(NeoMutt->sub, "markers");
395  if (!cnt && c_markers)
396  {
397  mutt_curses_set_color(MT_COLOR_MARKERS);
398  mutt_window_addch(win, '+');
399  last_color = mutt_color(MT_COLOR_MARKERS);
400  }
401  m = (line_info[n].syntax)[0].first;
402  cnt += (line_info[n].syntax)[0].last;
403  }
404  else
405  m = n;
406  if (flags & MUTT_PAGER_LOGS)
407  {
408  def_color = mutt_color(line_info[n].syntax[0].color);
409  }
410  else if (!(flags & MUTT_SHOWCOLOR))
411  def_color = mutt_color(MT_COLOR_NORMAL);
412  else if (line_info[m].type == MT_COLOR_HEADER)
413  def_color = line_info[m].syntax[0].color;
414  else
415  def_color = mutt_color(line_info[m].type);
416 
417  if ((flags & MUTT_SHOWCOLOR) && (line_info[m].type == MT_COLOR_QUOTED))
418  {
419  struct QClass *qc = line_info[m].quote;
420 
421  if (qc)
422  {
423  def_color = qc->color;
424 
425  while (qc && (qc->length > cnt))
426  {
427  def_color = qc->color;
428  qc = qc->up;
429  }
430  }
431  }
432 
433  color = def_color;
434  if ((flags & MUTT_SHOWCOLOR) && line_info[m].chunks)
435  {
436  matching_chunk = bsearch(&cnt, line_info[m].syntax, line_info[m].chunks,
437  sizeof(struct TextSyntax), comp_syntax_t);
438  if (matching_chunk && (cnt >= matching_chunk->first) &&
439  (cnt < matching_chunk->last))
440  {
441  color = matching_chunk->color;
442  }
443  }
444 
445  if ((flags & MUTT_SEARCH) && line_info[m].search_cnt)
446  {
447  matching_chunk = bsearch(&cnt, line_info[m].search, line_info[m].search_cnt,
448  sizeof(struct TextSyntax), comp_syntax_t);
449  if (matching_chunk && (cnt >= matching_chunk->first) &&
450  (cnt < matching_chunk->last))
451  {
452  color = mutt_color(MT_COLOR_SEARCH);
453  search = 1;
454  }
455  }
456 
457  /* handle "special" bold & underlined characters */
458  if (special || a->attr)
459  {
460 #ifdef HAVE_COLOR
461  if ((a->attr & ANSI_COLOR))
462  {
463  if (a->pair == -1)
464  a->pair = mutt_color_alloc(a->fg, a->bg);
465  color = a->pair;
466  if (a->attr & ANSI_BOLD)
467  color |= A_BOLD;
468  }
469  else
470 #endif
471  if ((special & A_BOLD) || (a->attr & ANSI_BOLD))
472  {
473  if (mutt_color(MT_COLOR_BOLD) && !search)
474  color = mutt_color(MT_COLOR_BOLD);
475  else
476  color ^= A_BOLD;
477  }
478  if ((special & A_UNDERLINE) || (a->attr & ANSI_UNDERLINE))
479  {
480  if (mutt_color(MT_COLOR_UNDERLINE) && !search)
481  color = mutt_color(MT_COLOR_UNDERLINE);
482  else
483  color ^= A_UNDERLINE;
484  }
485  else if (a->attr & ANSI_REVERSE)
486  {
487  color ^= A_REVERSE;
488  }
489  else if (a->attr & ANSI_BLINK)
490  {
491  color ^= A_BLINK;
492  }
493  else if (a->attr == ANSI_OFF)
494  {
495  a->attr = 0;
496  }
497  }
498 
499  if (color != last_color)
500  {
501  mutt_curses_set_attr(color);
502  last_color = color;
503  }
504 }

static struct QClass* classify_quote ( struct QClass **  quote_list,

Parameters

613 {
614  struct QClass *q_list = *quote_list;
615  struct QClass *qc = NULL, *tmp = NULL, *ptr = NULL, *save = NULL;
616  const char *tail_qptr = NULL;
617  size_t offset, tail_lng;
618  int index = -1;
619 
620  if (mutt_color_quotes_used() <= 1)
621  {
622  /* not much point in classifying quotes... */
623 
624  if (!*quote_list)
625  {
626  qc = mutt_mem_calloc(1, sizeof(struct QClass));
627  qc->color = mutt_color_quote(0);
628  *quote_list = qc;
629  }
630  return *quote_list;
631  }
632 
633  /* classify quoting prefix */
634  while (q_list)
635  {
636  if (length <= q_list->length)
637  {
638  /* case 1: check the top level nodes */
639 
640  if (mutt_strn_equal(qptr, q_list->prefix, length))
641  {
642  if (length == q_list->length)
643  return q_list; /* same prefix: return the current class */
644 
645  /* found shorter prefix */
646  if (!tmp)
647  {
648  /* add a node above q_list */
649  tmp = mutt_mem_calloc(1, sizeof(struct QClass));
650  tmp->prefix = mutt_mem_calloc(1, length + 1);
651  strncpy(tmp->prefix, qptr, length);
652  tmp->length = length;
653 
654  /* replace q_list by tmp in the top level list */
655  if (q_list->next)
656  {
657  tmp->next = q_list->next;
658  q_list->next->prev = tmp;
659  }
660  if (q_list->prev)
661  {
662  tmp->prev = q_list->prev;
663  q_list->prev->next = tmp;
664  }
665 
666  /* make q_list a child of tmp */
667  tmp->down = q_list;
668  q_list->up = tmp;
669 
670  /* q_list has no siblings for now */
671  q_list->next = NULL;
672  q_list->prev = NULL;
673 
674  /* update the root if necessary */
675  if (q_list == *quote_list)
676  *quote_list = tmp;
677 
678  index = q_list->index;
679 
680  /* tmp should be the return class too */
681  qc = tmp;
682 
683  /* next class to test; if tmp is a shorter prefix for another
684  * node, that node can only be in the top level list, so don't
685  * go down after this point */
686  q_list = tmp->next;
687  }
688  else
689  {
690  /* found another branch for which tmp is a shorter prefix */
691 
692  /* save the next sibling for later */
693  save = q_list->next;
694 
695  /* unlink q_list from the top level list */
696  if (q_list->next)
697  q_list->next->prev = q_list->prev;
698  if (q_list->prev)
699  q_list->prev->next = q_list->next;
700 
701  /* at this point, we have a tmp->down; link q_list to it */
702  ptr = tmp->down;
703  /* sibling order is important here, q_list should be linked last */
704  while (ptr->next)
705  ptr = ptr->next;
706  ptr->next = q_list;
707  q_list->next = NULL;
708  q_list->prev = ptr;
709  q_list->up = tmp;
710 
711  index = q_list->index;
712 
713  /* next class to test; as above, we shouldn't go down */
714  q_list = save;
715  }
716 
717  /* we found a shorter prefix, so certain quotes have changed classes */
718  *force_redraw = true;
719  continue;
720  }
721  else
722  {
723  /* shorter, but not a substring of the current class: try next */
724  q_list = q_list->next;
725  continue;
726  }
727  }
728  else
729  {
730  /* case 2: try subclassing the current top level node */
731 
732  /* tmp != NULL means we already found a shorter prefix at case 1 */
733  if (!tmp && mutt_strn_equal(qptr, q_list->prefix, q_list->length))
734  {
735  /* ok, it's a subclass somewhere on this branch */
736 
737  ptr = q_list;
738  offset = q_list->length;
739 
740  q_list = q_list->down;
741  tail_lng = length - offset;
742  tail_qptr = qptr + offset;
743 
744  while (q_list)
745  {
746  if (length <= q_list->length)
747  {
748  if (mutt_strn_equal(tail_qptr, (q_list->prefix) + offset, tail_lng))
749  {
750  /* same prefix: return the current class */
751  if (length == q_list->length)
752  return q_list;
753 
754  /* found shorter common prefix */
755  if (!tmp)
756  {
757  /* add a node above q_list */
758  tmp = mutt_mem_calloc(1, sizeof(struct QClass));
759  tmp->prefix = mutt_mem_calloc(1, length + 1);
760  strncpy(tmp->prefix, qptr, length);
761  tmp->length = length;
762 
763  /* replace q_list by tmp */
764  if (q_list->next)
765  {
766  tmp->next = q_list->next;
767  q_list->next->prev = tmp;
768  }
769  if (q_list->prev)
770  {
771  tmp->prev = q_list->prev;
772  q_list->prev->next = tmp;
773  }
774 
775  /* make q_list a child of tmp */
776  tmp->down = q_list;
777  tmp->up = q_list->up;
778  q_list->up = tmp;
779  if (tmp->up->down == q_list)
780  tmp->up->down = tmp;
781 
782  /* q_list has no siblings */
783  q_list->next = NULL;
784  q_list->prev = NULL;
785 
786  index = q_list->index;
787 
788  /* tmp should be the return class too */
789  qc = tmp;
790 
791  /* next class to test */
792  q_list = tmp->next;
793  }
794  else
795  {
796  /* found another branch for which tmp is a shorter prefix */
797 
798  /* save the next sibling for later */
799  save = q_list->next;
800 
801  /* unlink q_list from the top level list */
802  if (q_list->next)
803  q_list->next->prev = q_list->prev;
804  if (q_list->prev)
805  q_list->prev->next = q_list->next;
806 
807  /* at this point, we have a tmp->down; link q_list to it */
808  ptr = tmp->down;
809  while (ptr->next)
810  ptr = ptr->next;
811  ptr->next = q_list;
812  q_list->next = NULL;
813  q_list->prev = ptr;
814  q_list->up = tmp;
815 
816  index = q_list->index;
817 
818  /* next class to test */
819  q_list = save;
820  }
821 
822  /* we found a shorter prefix, so we need a redraw */
823  *force_redraw = true;
824  continue;
825  }
826  else
827  {
828  q_list = q_list->next;
829  continue;
830  }
831  }
832  else
833  {
834  /* longer than the current prefix: try subclassing it */
835  if (!tmp && mutt_strn_equal(tail_qptr, (q_list->prefix) + offset,
836  q_list->length - offset))
837  {
838  /* still a subclass: go down one level */
839  ptr = q_list;
840  offset = q_list->length;
841 
842  q_list = q_list->down;
843  tail_lng = length - offset;
844  tail_qptr = qptr + offset;
845 
846  continue;
847  }
848  else
849  {
850  /* nope, try the next prefix */
851  q_list = q_list->next;
852  continue;
853  }
854  }
855  }
856 
857  /* still not found so far: add it as a sibling to the current node */
858  if (!qc)
859  {
860  tmp = mutt_mem_calloc(1, sizeof(struct QClass));
861  tmp->prefix = mutt_mem_calloc(1, length + 1);
862  strncpy(tmp->prefix, qptr, length);
863  tmp->length = length;
864 
865  if (ptr->down)
866  {
867  tmp->next = ptr->down;
868  ptr->down->prev = tmp;
869  }
870  ptr->down = tmp;
871  tmp->up = ptr;
872 
873  class_color_new(tmp, q_level);
874 
875  return tmp;
876  }
877  else
878  {
879  if (index != -1)
880  shift_class_colors(*quote_list, tmp, index, q_level);
881 
882  return qc;
883  }
884  }
885  else
886  {
887  /* nope, try the next prefix */
888  q_list = q_list->next;
889  continue;
890  }
891  }
892  }
893 
894  if (!qc)
895  {
896  /* not found so far: add it as a top level class */
897  qc = mutt_mem_calloc(1, sizeof(struct QClass));
898  qc->prefix = mutt_mem_calloc(1, length + 1);
899  strncpy(qc->prefix, qptr, length);
900  qc->length = length;
901  class_color_new(qc, q_level);
902 
903  if (*quote_list)
904  {
905  qc->next = *quote_list;
906  (*quote_list)->prev = qc;
907  }
908  *quote_list = qc;
909  }
910 
911  if (index != -1)
912  shift_class_colors(*quote_list, tmp, index, q_level);
913 
914  return qc;
915 }

◆ mutt_is_quote_line()

int mutt_is_quote_line ( char *  line,
regmatch_t *  pmatch 
)

Is a line of message text a quote?

Parameters
[in]lineLine to test
[out]pmatchRegex sub-matches
Return values
trueLine is quoted

Checks if line matches the $quote_regex and doesn't match $smileys. This is used by the pager for calling classify_quote.

Definition at line 963 of file dlg_pager.c.

964 {
965  bool is_quote = false;
966  const struct Regex *c_smileys = cs_subset_regex(NeoMutt->sub, "smileys");
967  regmatch_t pmatch_internal[1], smatch[1];
968 
969  if (!pmatch)
970  pmatch = pmatch_internal;
971 
972  const struct Regex *c_quote_regex =
973  cs_subset_regex(NeoMutt->sub, "quote_regex");
974  if (mutt_regex_capture(c_quote_regex, line, 1, pmatch))
975  {
976  if (mutt_regex_capture(c_smileys, line, 1, smatch))
977  {
978  if (smatch[0].rm_so > 0)
979  {
980  char c = line[smatch[0].rm_so];
981  line[smatch[0].rm_so] = 0;
982 
983  if (mutt_regex_capture(c_quote_regex, line, 1, pmatch))
984  is_quote = true;
985 
986  line[smatch[0].rm_so] = c;
987  }
988  }
989  else
990  is_quote = true;
991  }
992 
993  return is_quote;
994 }

static void resolve_types ( struct MuttWindow *  win,

struct QClass **  quote_list,
int *  q_level,

)
static

Determine the style for a line of text.

Parameters
[in]winWindow
[in]bufFormatted text
[in]rawRaw text
[in]line_infoLine info array
[in]nLine number (index into line_info)
[in]lastLast line
[out]quote_listList of quote colours
[out]q_levelQuote level
[out]force_redrawSet to true if a screen redraw is needed
[in]q_classifyIf true, style the text

Definition at line 1009 of file dlg_pager.c.

1012 {
1013  struct ColorLine *color_line = NULL;
1014  struct ColorLineList *head = NULL;
1015  regmatch_t pmatch[1];
1016  bool found;
1017  bool null_rx;
1018  const bool c_header_color_partial =
1019  cs_subset_bool(NeoMutt->sub, "header_color_partial");
1020  int offset, i = 0;
1021 
1022  if ((n == 0) || mutt_color_is_header(line_info[n - 1].type) ||
1023  (check_protected_header_marker(raw) == 0))
1024  {
1025  if (buf[0] == '\n') /* end of header */
1026  {
1027  line_info[n].type = MT_COLOR_NORMAL;
1028  mutt_window_get_coords(win, &braille_col, &braille_row);
1029  }
1030  else
1031  {
1032  /* if this is a continuation of the previous line, use the previous
1033  * line's color as default. */
1034  if ((n > 0) && ((buf[0] == ' ') || (buf[0] == '\t')))
1035  {
1036  line_info[n].type = line_info[n - 1].type; /* wrapped line */
1037  if (!c_header_color_partial)
1038  {
1039  (line_info[n].syntax)[0].color = (line_info[n - 1].syntax)[0].color;
1040  line_info[n].is_cont_hdr = 1;
1041  }
1042  }
1043  else
1044  {
1045  line_info[n].type = MT_COLOR_HDRDEFAULT;
1046  }
1047 
1048  /* When this option is unset, we color the entire header the
1049  * same color. Otherwise, we handle the header patterns just
1050  * like body patterns (further below). */
1051  if (!c_header_color_partial)
1052  {
1053  STAILQ_FOREACH(color_line, mutt_color_headers(), entries)
1054  {
1055  if (regexec(&color_line->regex, buf, 0, NULL, 0) == 0)
1056  {
1057  line_info[n].type = MT_COLOR_HEADER;
1058  line_info[n].syntax[0].color = color_line->pair;
1059  if (line_info[n].is_cont_hdr)
1060  {
1061  /* adjust the previous continuation lines to reflect the color of this continuation line */
1062  int j;
1063  for (j = n - 1; j >= 0 && line_info[j].is_cont_hdr; --j)
1064  {
1065  line_info[j].type = line_info[n].type;
1066  line_info[j].syntax[0].color = line_info[n].syntax[0].color;
1067  }
1068  /* now adjust the first line of this header field */
1069  if (j >= 0)
1070  {
1071  line_info[j].type = line_info[n].type;
1072  line_info[j].syntax[0].color = line_info[n].syntax[0].color;
1073  }
1074  *force_redraw = true; /* the previous lines have already been drawn on the screen */
1075  }
1076  break;
1077  }
1078  }
1079  }
1080  }
1081  }
1082  else if (mutt_str_startswith(raw, "\033[0m")) // Escape: a little hack...
1083  line_info[n].type = MT_COLOR_NORMAL;
1084  else if (check_attachment_marker((char *) raw) == 0)
1085  line_info[n].type = MT_COLOR_ATTACHMENT;
1086  else if (mutt_str_equal("-- \n", buf) || mutt_str_equal("-- \r\n", buf))
1087  {
1088  i = n + 1;
1089 
1090  line_info[n].type = MT_COLOR_SIGNATURE;
1091  while ((i < last) && (check_sig(buf, line_info, i - 1) == 0) &&
1092  ((line_info[i].type == MT_COLOR_NORMAL) || (line_info[i].type == MT_COLOR_QUOTED) ||
1093  (line_info[i].type == MT_COLOR_HEADER)))
1094  {
1095  /* oops... */
1096  if (line_info[i].chunks)
1097  {
1098  line_info[i].chunks = 0;
1099  mutt_mem_realloc(&(line_info[n].syntax), sizeof(struct TextSyntax));
1100  }
1101  line_info[i++].type = MT_COLOR_SIGNATURE;
1102  }
1103  }
1104  else if (check_sig(buf, line_info, n - 1) == 0)
1105  line_info[n].type = MT_COLOR_SIGNATURE;
1106  else if (mutt_is_quote_line(buf, pmatch))
1107 
1108  {
1109  if (q_classify && (line_info[n].quote == NULL))
1110  {
1111  line_info[n].quote = classify_quote(quote_list, buf + pmatch[0].rm_so,
1112  pmatch[0].rm_eo - pmatch[0].rm_so,
1113  force_redraw, q_level);
1114  }
1115  line_info[n].type = MT_COLOR_QUOTED;
1116  }
1117  else
1118  line_info[n].type = MT_COLOR_NORMAL;
1119 
1120  /* body patterns */
1121  if ((line_info[n].type == MT_COLOR_NORMAL) || (line_info[n].type == MT_COLOR_QUOTED) ||
1122  ((line_info[n].type == MT_COLOR_HDRDEFAULT) && c_header_color_partial))
1123  {
1124  size_t nl;
1125 
1126  /* don't consider line endings part of the buffer
1127  * for regex matching */
1128  nl = mutt_str_len(buf);
1129  if ((nl > 0) && (buf[nl - 1] == '\n'))
1130  buf[nl - 1] = '\0';
1131 
1132  i = 0;
1133  offset = 0;
1134  line_info[n].chunks = 0;
1135  if (line_info[n].type == MT_COLOR_HDRDEFAULT)
1136  head = mutt_color_headers();
1137  else
1138  head = mutt_color_body();
1139  STAILQ_FOREACH(color_line, head, entries)
1140  {
1141  color_line->stop_matching = false;
1142  }
1143  do
1144  {
1145  if (!buf[offset])
1146  break;
1147 
1148  found = false;
1149  null_rx = false;
1150  STAILQ_FOREACH(color_line, head, entries)
1151  {
1152  if (!color_line->stop_matching &&
1153  (regexec(&color_line->regex, buf + offset, 1, pmatch,
1154  ((offset != 0) ? REG_NOTBOL : 0)) == 0))
1155  {
1156  if (pmatch[0].rm_eo != pmatch[0].rm_so)
1157  {
1158  if (!found)
1159  {
1160  /* Abort if we fill up chunks.
1161  * Yes, this really happened. */
1162  if (line_info[n].chunks == SHRT_MAX)
1163  {
1164  null_rx = false;
1165  break;
1166  }
1167  if (++(line_info[n].chunks) > 1)
1168  {
1169  mutt_mem_realloc(&(line_info[n].syntax),
1170  (line_info[n].chunks) * sizeof(struct TextSyntax));
1171  }
1172  }
1173  i = line_info[n].chunks - 1;
1174  pmatch[0].rm_so += offset;
1175  pmatch[0].rm_eo += offset;
1176  if (!found || (pmatch[0].rm_so < (line_info[n].syntax)[i].first) ||
1177  ((pmatch[0].rm_so == (line_info[n].syntax)[i].first) &&
1178  (pmatch[0].rm_eo > (line_info[n].syntax)[i].last)))
1179  {
1180  (line_info[n].syntax)[i].color = color_line->pair;
1181  (line_info[n].syntax)[i].first = pmatch[0].rm_so;
1182  (line_info[n].syntax)[i].last = pmatch[0].rm_eo;
1183  }
1184  found = true;
1185  null_rx = false;
1186  }
1187  else
1188  null_rx = true; /* empty regex; don't add it, but keep looking */
1189  }
1190  else
1191  {
1192  /* Once a regexp fails to match, don't try matching it again.
1193  * On very long lines this can cause a performance issue if there
1194  * are other regexps that have many matches. */
1195  color_line->stop_matching = true;
1196  }
1197  }
1198 
1199  if (null_rx)
1200  offset++; /* avoid degenerate cases */
1201  else
1202  offset = (line_info[n].syntax)[i].last;
1203  } while (found || null_rx);
1204  if (nl > 0)
1205  buf[nl] = '\n';
1206  }
1207 
1208  /* attachment patterns */
1209  if (line_info[n].type == MT_COLOR_ATTACHMENT)
1210  {
1211  size_t nl;
1212 
1213  /* don't consider line endings part of the buffer for regex matching */
1214  nl = mutt_str_len(buf);
1215  if ((nl > 0) && (buf[nl - 1] == '\n'))
1216  buf[nl - 1] = '\0';
1217 
1218  i = 0;
1219  offset = 0;
1220  line_info[n].chunks = 0;
1221  do
1222  {
1223  if (!buf[offset])
1224  break;
1225 
1226  found = false;
1227  null_rx = false;
1228  STAILQ_FOREACH(color_line, mutt_color_attachments(), entries)
1229  {
1230  if (regexec(&color_line->regex, buf + offset, 1, pmatch,
1231  ((offset != 0) ? REG_NOTBOL : 0)) == 0)
1232  {
1233  if (pmatch[0].rm_eo != pmatch[0].rm_so)
1234  {
1235  if (!found)
1236  {
1237  if (++(line_info[n].chunks) > 1)
1238  {
1239  mutt_mem_realloc(&(line_info[n].syntax),
1240  (line_info[n].chunks) * sizeof(struct TextSyntax));
1241  }
1242  }
1243  i = line_info[n].chunks - 1;
1244  pmatch[0].rm_so += offset;
1245  pmatch[0].rm_eo += offset;
1246  if (!found || (pmatch[0].rm_so < (line_info[n].syntax)[i].first) ||
1247  ((pmatch[0].rm_so == (line_info[n].syntax)[i].first) &&
1248  (pmatch[0].rm_eo > (line_info[n].syntax)[i].last)))
1249  {
1250  (line_info[n].syntax)[i].color = color_line->pair;
1251  (line_info[n].syntax)[i].first = pmatch[0].rm_so;
1252  (line_info[n].syntax)[i].last = pmatch[0].rm_eo;
1253  }
1254  found = 1;
1255  null_rx = 0;
1256  }
1257  else
1258  null_rx = 1; /* empty regex; don't add it, but keep looking */
1259  }
1260  }
1261 
1262  if (null_rx)
1263  offset++; /* avoid degenerate cases */
1264  else
1265  offset = (line_info[n].syntax)[i].last;
1266  } while (found || null_rx);
1267  if (nl > 0)
1268  buf[nl] = '\n';
1269  }
1270 }

1301  {

1304 #ifdef HAVE_COLOR
1305  if (a->pair != -1)

1316  pos += 2;
1317  }
1318  else if ((buf[pos] == '4') && ((pos + 1 == x) || (buf[pos + 1] == ';')))
1319  {
1320  a->attr |= ANSI_UNDERLINE;
1321  pos += 2;
1322  }
1323  else if ((buf[pos] == '5') && ((pos + 1 == x) || (buf[pos + 1] == ';')))
1324  {
1325  a->attr |= ANSI_BLINK;
1326  pos += 2;
1327  }

1329  {

1337  mutt_color_free(a->fg, a->bg);

1340  a->pair = -1;
1341  pos += 2;
1342  }

1344  {
1345 #ifdef HAVE_COLOR
1346  if (a->pair != -1)
1347  mutt_color_free(a->fg, a->bg);
1348 #endif
1349  a->pair = -1;
1350  a->attr |= ANSI_COLOR;

1352  pos += 3;

1355  {
1356 #ifdef HAVE_COLOR

1359 #endif

1368  pos++;
1369  pos++;
1370  }
1371  }
1372  }

◆ mutt_buffer_strip_formatting()

void mutt_buffer_strip_formatting ( struct Buffer *  dest,
const char *  src,
bool  strip_markers 
)

Removes ANSI and backspace formatting.

Parameters
destBuffer for the result
srcString to strip
strip_markersRemove

Removes ANSI and backspace formatting, and optionally markers. This is separated out so that it can be used both by the pager and the autoview handler.

This logic is pulled from the pager fill_buffer() function, for use in stripping reply-quoted autoview output of ansi sequences.

Definition at line 1390 of file dlg_pager.c.

1391 {
1392  const char *s = src;
1393 
1394  mutt_buffer_reset(dest);
1395 
1396  if (!s)
1397  return;
1398 
1399  while (s[0] != '\0')
1400  {
1401  if ((s[0] == '\010') && (s > src))
1402  {
1403  if (s[1] == '_') /* underline */
1404  s += 2;
1405  else if (s[1] && mutt_buffer_len(dest)) /* bold or overstrike */
1406  {
1407  dest->dptr--;
1408  mutt_buffer_addch(dest, s[1]);
1409  s += 2;
1410  }
1411  else /* ^H */
1412  mutt_buffer_addch(dest, *s++);
1413  }
1414  else if ((s[0] == '\033') && (s[1] == '[') && is_ansi(s + 2))
1415  {
1416  while (*s++ != 'm')
1417  ; /* skip ANSI sequence */
1418  }
1419  else if (strip_markers && (s[0] == '\033') && (s[1] == ']') &&
1420  ((check_attachment_marker(s) == 0) || (check_protected_header_marker(s) == 0)))
1421  {
1422  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG2, "Seen attachment marker\n");
1423  while (*s++ != '\a')
1424  ; /* skip pseudo-ANSI sequence */
1425  }
1426  else
1427  mutt_buffer_addch(dest, *s++);
1428  }
1429 }

Fill a buffer from a file.

Parameters
[in]fpFile to read from
[in,out]last_posEnd of last read
[in]offsetPosition start reading from
[out]bufBuffer to fill
[out]fmtCopy of buffer, stripped of attributes
[out]blenLength of the buffer
[in,out]buf_readytrue if the buffer already has data in it
Return values

-1Error

1452  fseeko(fp, offset, SEEK_SET);
1453

1456  {

1458  return -1;

1460

1469  FREE(fmt);
1470  *fmt = (unsigned char *) stripped.data;
1471  }

1496  int space = -1; /* index of the last space or TAB */
1497  const bool c_markers = cs_subset_bool(NeoMutt->sub, "markers");
1498  size_t col = c_markers ? (*line_info)[n].continuation : 0;
1499  size_t k;
1500  int ch, vch, last_special = -1, special = 0, t;
1501  wchar_t wc;
1502  mbstate_t mbstate;
1503  const size_t c_wrap = cs_subset_number(NeoMutt->sub, "wrap");
1504  size_t wrap_cols = mutt_window_wrap_cols(width, (flags & MUTT_PAGER_NOWRAP) ? 0 : c_wrap);
1505 
1506  if (check_attachment_marker((char *) buf) == 0)
1507  wrap_cols = width;
1508 
1509  /* FIXME: this should come from line_info */
1510  memset(&mbstate, 0, sizeof(mbstate));
1511 
1512  for (ch = 0, vch = 0; ch < cnt; ch += k, vch += k)
1513  {
1514  /* Handle ANSI sequences */
1515  while ((cnt - ch >= 2) && (buf[ch] == '\033') && (buf[ch + 1] == '[') && // Escape
1516  is_ansi((char *) buf + ch + 2))
1517  {
1518  ch = grok_ansi(buf, ch + 2, pa) + 1;
1519  }
1520 
1521  while ((cnt - ch >= 2) && (buf[ch] == '\033') && (buf[ch + 1] == ']') && // Escape
1522  ((check_attachment_marker((char *) buf + ch) == 0) ||
1523  (check_protected_header_marker((char *) buf + ch) == 0)))
1524  {
1525  while (buf[ch++] != '\a')
1526  if (ch >= cnt)
1527  break;
1528  }
1529 
1530  /* is anything left to do? */
1531  if (ch >= cnt)
1532  break;
1533 
1534  k = mbrtowc(&wc, (char *) buf + ch, cnt - ch, &mbstate);
1535  if ((k == (size_t) (-2)) || (k == (size_t) (-1)))
1536  {
1537  if (k == (size_t) (-1))
1538  memset(&mbstate, 0, sizeof(mbstate));
1539  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG1, "mbrtowc returned %lu; errno = %d\n", k, errno);
1540  if (col + 4 > wrap_cols)
1541  break;
1542  col += 4;
1543  if (pa)
1544  mutt_window_printf(win, "\\%03o", buf[ch]);
1545  k = 1;
1546  continue;
1547  }
1548  if (k == 0)
1549  k = 1;
1550 
1551  if (CharsetIsUtf8)
1552  {
1553  /* zero width space, zero width no-break space */
1554  if ((wc == 0x200B) || (wc == 0xFEFF))
1555  {
1556  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG3, "skip zero-width character U+%04X\n", (unsigned short) wc);
1557  continue;
1558  }
1559  if (mutt_mb_is_display_corrupting_utf8(wc))
1560  {
1561  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG3, "filtered U+%04X\n", (unsigned short) wc);
1562  continue;
1563  }
1564  }
1565 
1566  /* Handle backspace */
1567  special = 0;
1568  if (IsWPrint(wc))
1569  {
1570  wchar_t wc1;
1571  mbstate_t mbstate1 = mbstate;
1572  size_t k1 = mbrtowc(&wc1, (char *) buf + ch + k, cnt - ch - k, &mbstate1);
1573  while ((k1 != (size_t) (-2)) && (k1 != (size_t) (-1)) && (k1 > 0) && (wc1 == '\b'))
1574  {
1575  const size_t k2 =
1576  mbrtowc(&wc1, (char *) buf + ch + k + k1, cnt - ch - k - k1, &mbstate1);
1577  if ((k2 == (size_t) (-2)) || (k2 == (size_t) (-1)) || (k2 == 0) || (!IsWPrint(wc1)))
1578  break;
1579 
1580  if (wc == wc1)
1581  {
1582  special |= ((wc == '_') && (special & A_UNDERLINE)) ? A_UNDERLINE : A_BOLD;
1583  }
1584  else if ((wc == '_') || (wc1 == '_'))
1585  {
1586  special |= A_UNDERLINE;
1587  wc = (wc1 == '_') ? wc : wc1;
1588  }
1589  else
1590  {
1591  /* special = 0; / * overstrike: nothing to do! */
1592  wc = wc1;
1593  }
1594 
1595  ch += k + k1;
1596  k = k2;
1597  mbstate = mbstate1;
1598  k1 = mbrtowc(&wc1, (char *) buf + ch + k, cnt - ch - k, &mbstate1);
1599  }
1600  }
1601 
1602  if (pa && ((flags & (MUTT_SHOWCOLOR | MUTT_SEARCH | MUTT_PAGER_MARKER)) ||
1603  special || last_special || pa->attr))
1604  {
1605  resolve_color(win, *line_info, n, vch, flags, special, pa);
1606  last_special = special;
1607  }
1608 
1609  /* no-break space, narrow no-break space */
1610  if (IsWPrint(wc) || (CharsetIsUtf8 && ((wc == 0x00A0) || (wc == 0x202F))))
1611  {
1612  if (wc == ' ')
1613  {
1614  space = ch;
1615  }
1616  t = wcwidth(wc);
1617  if (col + t > wrap_cols)
1618  break;
1619  col += t;
1620  if (pa)
1621  mutt_addwch(win, wc);
1622  }
1623  else if (wc == '\n')
1624  break;
1625  else if (wc == '\t')
1626  {
1627  space = ch;
1628  t = (col & ~7) + 8;
1629  if (t > wrap_cols)
1630  break;
1631  if (pa)
1632  for (; col < t; col++)
1633  mutt_window_addch(win, ' ');
1634  else
1635  col = t;
1636  }
1637  else if ((wc < 0x20) || (wc == 0x7f))
1638  {
1639  if (col + 2 > wrap_cols)
1640  break;
1641  col += 2;
1642  if (pa)
1643  mutt_window_printf(win, "^%c", ('@' + wc) & 0x7f);
1644  }
1645  else if (wc < 0x100)
1646  {
1647  if (col + 4 > wrap_cols)
1648  break;
1649  col += 4;
1650  if (pa)
1651  mutt_window_printf(win, "\\%03o", wc);
1652  }
1653  else
1654  {
1655  if (col + 1 > wrap_cols)
1656  break;
1657  col += k;
1658  if (pa)
1659  mutt_addwch(win, ReplacementChar);
1660  }
1661  }
1662  *pspace = space;
1663  *pcol = col;
1664  *pvch = vch;
1665  *pspecial = special;
1666  return ch;
1667 }

Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ display_line()

static int display_line ( FILE *  fp,
LOFF_T *  last_pos,
struct Line **  line_info,
int  n,
int *  last,
int *  max,
PagerFlags  flags,
struct QClass **  quote_list,
int *  q_level,
bool *  force_redraw,
regex_t *  search_re,
struct MuttWindow *  win_pager 
)
static

Print a line on screen.

Parameters
[in]fpFile to read from
[out]last_posOffset into file
[out]line_infoLine attributes
[in]nLine number
[out]lastLast line
[out]maxMaximum number of lines
[in]flagsFlags, see PagerFlags
[out]quote_listEmail quoting style
[out]q_levelLevel of quoting
[out]force_redrawForce a repaint
[out]search_reRegex to highlight
[in]win_pagerWindow to draw into
Return values
-1EOF was reached
0normal exit, line was not displayed
>0normal exit, line was displayed

Definition at line 1687 of file dlg_pager.c.

1691 {
1692  unsigned char *buf = NULL, *fmt = NULL;
1693  size_t buflen = 0;
1694  unsigned char *buf_ptr = NULL;
1695  int ch, vch, col, cnt, b_read;
1696  int buf_ready = 0;
1697  bool change_last = false;
1698  const bool c_smart_wrap = cs_subset_bool(NeoMutt->sub, "smart_wrap");
1699  int special;
1700  int offset;
1701  int def_color;
1702  int m;
1703  int rc = -1;
1704  struct AnsiAttr a = { 0, 0, 0, -1 };
1705  regmatch_t pmatch[1];
1706 
1707  if (n == *last)
1708  {
1709  (*last)++;
1710  change_last = true;
1711  }
1712 
1713  if (*last == *max)
1714  {
1715  mutt_mem_realloc(line_info, sizeof(struct Line) * (*max += LINES));
1716  for (ch = *last; ch < *max; ch++)
1717  {
1718  memset(&((*line_info)[ch]), 0, sizeof(struct Line));
1719  (*line_info)[ch].type = -1;
1720  (*line_info)[ch].search_cnt = -1;
1721  (*line_info)[ch].syntax = mutt_mem_malloc(sizeof(struct TextSyntax));
1722  ((*line_info)[ch].syntax)[0].first = -1;
1723  ((*line_info)[ch].syntax)[0].last = -1;
1724  }
1725  }
1726 
1727  struct Line *const curr_line = &(*line_info)[n];
1728 
1729  if (flags & MUTT_PAGER_LOGS)
1730  {
1731  /* determine the line class */
1732  if (fill_buffer(fp, last_pos, curr_line->offset, &buf, &fmt, &buflen, &buf_ready) < 0)
1733  {
1734  if (change_last)
1735  (*last)--;
1736  goto out;
1737  }
1738 
1739  curr_line->type = MT_COLOR_MESSAGE_LOG;
1740  if (buf[11] == 'M')
1741  curr_line->syntax[0].color = MT_COLOR_MESSAGE;
1742  else if (buf[11] == 'W')
1743  curr_line->syntax[0].color = MT_COLOR_WARNING;
1744  else if (buf[11] == 'E')
1745  curr_line->syntax[0].color = MT_COLOR_ERROR;
1746  else
1747  curr_line->syntax[0].color = MT_COLOR_NORMAL;
1748  }
1749 
1750  /* only do color highlighting if we are viewing a message */
1751  if (flags & (MUTT_SHOWCOLOR | MUTT_TYPES))
1752  {
1753  if (curr_line->type == -1)
1754  {
1755  /* determine the line class */
1756  if (fill_buffer(fp, last_pos, curr_line->offset, &buf, &fmt, &buflen, &buf_ready) < 0)
1757  {
1758  if (change_last)
1759  (*last)--;
1760  goto out;
1761  }
1762 
1763  resolve_types(win_pager, (char *) fmt, (char *) buf, *line_info, n, *last,
1764  quote_list, q_level, force_redraw, flags & MUTT_SHOWCOLOR);
1765 
1766  /* avoid race condition for continuation lines when scrolling up */
1767  for (m = n + 1; m < *last && (*line_info)[m].offset && (*line_info)[m].continuation; m++)
1768  (*line_info)[m].type = curr_line->type;
1769  }
1770 
1771  /* this also prevents searching through the hidden lines */
1772  const short c_toggle_quoted_show_levels =
1773  cs_subset_number(NeoMutt->sub, "toggle_quoted_show_levels");
1774  if ((flags & MUTT_HIDE) && (curr_line->type == MT_COLOR_QUOTED) &&
1775  ((curr_line->quote == NULL) || (curr_line->quote->index >= c_toggle_quoted_show_levels)))
1776  {
1777  flags = 0; /* MUTT_NOSHOW */
1778  }
1779  }
1780 
1781  /* At this point, (*line_info[n]).quote may still be undefined. We
1782  * don't want to compute it every time MUTT_TYPES is set, since this
1783  * would slow down the "bottom" function unacceptably. A compromise
1784  * solution is hence to call regexec() again, just to find out the
1785  * length of the quote prefix. */
1786  if ((flags & MUTT_SHOWCOLOR) && !curr_line->continuation &&
1787  (curr_line->type == MT_COLOR_QUOTED) && !curr_line->quote)
1788  {
1789  if (fill_buffer(fp, last_pos, curr_line->offset, &buf, &fmt, &buflen, &buf_ready) < 0)
1790  {
1791  if (change_last)
1792  (*last)--;
1793  goto out;
1794  }
1795 
1796  const struct Regex *c_quote_regex =
1797  cs_subset_regex(NeoMutt->sub, "quote_regex");
1798  if (mutt_regex_capture(c_quote_regex, (char *) fmt, 1, pmatch))
1799  {
1800  curr_line->quote =
1801  classify_quote(quote_list, (char *) fmt + pmatch[0].rm_so,
1802  pmatch[0].rm_eo - pmatch[0].rm_so, force_redraw, q_level);
1803  }
1804  else
1805  {
1806  goto out;
1807  }
1808  }
1809 
1810  if ((flags & MUTT_SEARCH) && !curr_line->continuation && (curr_line->search_cnt == -1))
1811  {
1812  if (fill_buffer(fp, last_pos, curr_line->offset, &buf, &fmt, &buflen, &buf_ready) < 0)
1813  {
1814  if (change_last)
1815  (*last)--;
1816  goto out;
1817  }
1818 
1819  offset = 0;
1820  curr_line->search_cnt = 0;
1821  while (regexec(search_re, (char *) fmt + offset, 1, pmatch,
1822  (offset ? REG_NOTBOL : 0)) == 0)
1823  {
1824  if (++(curr_line->search_cnt) > 1)
1825  {
1826  mutt_mem_realloc(&(curr_line->search),
1827  (curr_line->search_cnt) * sizeof(struct TextSyntax));
1828  }
1829  else
1830  curr_line->search = mutt_mem_malloc(sizeof(struct TextSyntax));
1831  pmatch[0].rm_so += offset;
1832  pmatch[0].rm_eo += offset;
1833  (curr_line->search)[curr_line->search_cnt - 1].first = pmatch[0].rm_so;
1834  (curr_line->search)[curr_line->search_cnt - 1].last = pmatch[0].rm_eo;
1835 
1836  if (pmatch[0].rm_eo == pmatch[0].rm_so)
1837  offset++; /* avoid degenerate cases */
1838  else
1839  offset = pmatch[0].rm_eo;
1840  if (!fmt[offset])
1841  break;
1842  }
1843  }
1844 
1845  if (!(flags & MUTT_SHOW) && ((*line_info)[n + 1].offset > 0))
1846  {
1847  /* we've already scanned this line, so just exit */
1848  rc = 0;
1849  goto out;
1850  }
1851  if ((flags & MUTT_SHOWCOLOR) && *force_redraw && ((*line_info)[n + 1].offset > 0))
1852  {
1853  /* no need to try to display this line... */
1854  rc = 1;
1855  goto out; /* fake display */
1856  }
1857 
1858  b_read = fill_buffer(fp, last_pos, curr_line->offset, &buf, &fmt, &buflen, &buf_ready);
1859  if (b_read < 0)
1860  {
1861  if (change_last)
1862  (*last)--;
1863  goto out;
1864  }
1865 
1866  /* now chose a good place to break the line */
1867  cnt = format_line(win_pager, line_info, n, buf, flags, NULL, b_read, &ch,
1868  &vch, &col, &special, win_pager->state.cols);
1869  buf_ptr = buf + cnt;
1870 
1871  /* move the break point only if smart_wrap is set */
1872  if (c_smart_wrap)
1873  {
1874  if ((cnt < b_read) && (ch != -1) &&
1875  !mutt_color_is_header(curr_line->type) && !IS_SPACE(buf[cnt]))
1876  {
1877  buf_ptr = buf + ch;
1878  /* skip trailing blanks */
1879  while (ch && ((buf[ch] == ' ') || (buf[ch] == '\t') || (buf[ch] == '\r')))
1880  ch--;
1881  /* A very long word with leading spaces causes infinite
1882  * wrapping when MUTT_PAGER_NSKIP is set. A folded header
1883  * with a single long word shouldn't be smartwrapped
1884  * either. So just disable smart_wrap if it would wrap at the
1885  * beginning of the line. */
1886  if (ch == 0)
1887  buf_ptr = buf + cnt;
1888  else
1889  cnt = ch + 1;
1890  }
1891  if (!(flags & MUTT_PAGER_NSKIP))
1892  {
1893  /* skip leading blanks on the next line too */
1894  while ((*buf_ptr == ' ') || (*buf_ptr == '\t'))
1895  buf_ptr++;
1896  }
1897  }
1898 
1899  if (*buf_ptr == '\r')
1900  buf_ptr++;
1901  if (*buf_ptr == '\n')
1902  buf_ptr++;
1903 
1904  if (((int) (buf_ptr - buf) < b_read) && !(*line_info)[n + 1].continuation)
1905  append_line(*line_info, n, (int) (buf_ptr - buf));
1906  (*line_info)[n + 1].offset = curr_line->offset + (long) (buf_ptr - buf);
1907 
1908  /* if we don't need to display the line we are done */
1909  if (!(flags & MUTT_SHOW))
1910  {
1911  rc = 0;
1912  goto out;
1913  }
1914 
1915  /* display the line */
1916  format_line(win_pager, line_info, n, buf, flags, &a, cnt, &ch, &vch, &col,
1917  &special, win_pager->state.cols);
1918 
1919 /* avoid a bug in ncurses... */
1920 #ifndef USE_SLANG_CURSES
1921  if (col == 0)
1922  {
1923  mutt_curses_set_color(MT_COLOR_NORMAL);
1924  mutt_window_addch(win_pager, ' ');
1925  }
1926 #endif
1927 
1928  /* end the last color pattern (needed by S-Lang) */
1929  if (special || ((col != win_pager->state.cols) && (flags & (MUTT_SHOWCOLOR | MUTT_SEARCH))))
1930  resolve_color(win_pager, *line_info, n, vch, flags, 0, &a);
1931 
1932  /* Fill the blank space at the end of the line with the prevailing color.
1933  * ncurses does an implicit clrtoeol() when you do mutt_window_addch('\n') so we have
1934  * to make sure to reset the color *after* that */
1935  if (flags & MUTT_SHOWCOLOR)
1936  {
1937  m = (curr_line->continuation) ? (curr_line->syntax)[0].first : n;
1938  if ((*line_info)[m].type == MT_COLOR_HEADER)
1939  def_color = ((*line_info)[m].syntax)[0].color;
1940  else
1941  def_color = mutt_color((*line_info)[m].type);
1942 
1943  mutt_curses_set_attr(def_color);
1944  }
1945 
1946  if (col < win_pager->state.cols)
1947  mutt_window_clrtoeol(win_pager);
1948 
1949  /* reset the color back to normal. This *must* come after the
1950  * clrtoeol, otherwise the color for this line will not be
1951  * filled to the right margin. */
1952  if (flags & MUTT_SHOWCOLOR)
1953  mutt_curses_set_color(MT_COLOR_NORMAL);
1954 
1955  /* build a return code */
1956  if (!(flags & MUTT_SHOW))
1957  flags = 0;
1958 
1959  rc = flags;
1960 
1961 out:
1962  FREE(&buf);
1963  FREE(&fmt);
1964  return rc;
1965 }

static int up_n_lines ( int  nlines,

int  cur,

)
static

Reposition the pager's view up by n lines.

Parameters
nlinesNumber of lines to move
infoLine info array
curCurrent line number
hidingtrue if lines have been hidden

numNew current line number

◆ pager_custom_redraw()

static void pager_custom_redraw ( struct Menu *  pager_menu)
static

Redraw the pager window - Implements Menu::custom_redraw()

Definition at line 1999 of file dlg_pager.c.

2000 {
2001  //---------------------------------------------------------------------------
2002  // ASSUMPTIONS & SANITY CHECKS
2003  //---------------------------------------------------------------------------
2004  // Since pager_custom_redraw() is a static function and it is always called
2005  // after mutt_pager() we can rely on a series of sanity checks in
2006  // mutt_pager(), namely:
2007  // - PAGER_MODE_EMAIL guarantees ( data->email) and (!data->body)
2008  // - PAGER_MODE_ATTACH guarantees ( data->email) and ( data->body)
2009  // - PAGER_MODE_OTHER guarantees (!data->email) and (!data->body)
2010  //
2011  // Additionally, while refactoring is still in progress the following checks
2012  // are still here to ensure data model consistency.
2013  assert(pager_menu);
2014  struct PagerPrivateData *priv = pager_menu->mdata;
2015  assert(priv); // Redraw function can't be called without it's data.
2016  assert(priv->pview); // Redraw data can't exist separately without the view.
2017  assert(priv->pview->pdata); // View can't exist without it's data
2018  //---------------------------------------------------------------------------
2019 
2020  char buf[1024] = { 0 };
2021  struct IndexSharedData *shared = dialog_find(priv->pview->win_pager)->wdata;
2022  struct Mailbox *m = priv->pview->pdata->ctx ? priv->pview->pdata->ctx->mailbox : NULL;
2023 
2024  const bool c_tilde = cs_subset_bool(NeoMutt->sub, "tilde");
2025  const short c_pager_index_lines =
2026  cs_subset_number(NeoMutt->sub, "pager_index_lines");
2027 
2028  if (pager_menu->redraw & MENU_REDRAW_FULL)
2029  {
2030  mutt_curses_set_color(MT_COLOR_NORMAL);
2031  mutt_window_clear(priv->pview->win_pager);
2032 
2033  if ((priv->pview->mode == PAGER_MODE_EMAIL) && ((m->vcount + 1) < c_pager_index_lines))
2034  {
2035  priv->indexlen = m->vcount + 1;
2036  }
2037  else
2038  {
2039  priv->indexlen = c_pager_index_lines;
2040  }
2041 
2042  priv->indicator = priv->indexlen / 3;
2043 
2044  if (Resize)
2045  {
2046  priv->search_compiled = Resize->search_compiled;
2047  if (priv->search_compiled)
2048  {
2049  uint16_t flags = mutt_mb_is_lower(priv->searchbuf) ? REG_ICASE : 0;
2050  const int err = REG_COMP(&priv->search_re, priv->searchbuf, REG_NEWLINE | flags);
2051  if (err == 0)
2052  {
2053  priv->search_flag = MUTT_SEARCH;
2054  priv->search_back = Resize->search_back;
2055  }
2056  else
2057  {
2058  regerror(err, &priv->search_re, buf, sizeof(buf));
2059  mutt_error("%s", buf);
2060  priv->search_compiled = false;
2061  }
2062  }
2063  priv->lines = Resize->line;
2064  menu_queue_redraw(pager_menu, MENU_REDRAW_FLOW);
2065 
2066  FREE(&Resize);
2067  }
2068 
2069  if ((priv->pview->mode == PAGER_MODE_EMAIL) && (c_pager_index_lines != 0) && priv->menu)
2070  {
2071  mutt_curses_set_color(MT_COLOR_NORMAL);
2072 
2073  /* some fudge to work out whereabouts the indicator should go */
2074  const int index = menu_get_index(priv->menu);
2075  if ((index - priv->indicator) < 0)
2076  priv->menu->top = 0;
2077  else if ((priv->menu->max - index) < (priv->menu->pagelen - priv->indicator))
2078  priv->menu->top = priv->menu->max - priv->menu->pagelen;
2079  else
2080  priv->menu->top = index - priv->indicator;
2081 
2082  menu_redraw_index(priv->menu);
2083  }
2084 
2085  menu_queue_redraw(pager_menu, MENU_REDRAW_BODY | MENU_REDRAW_INDEX | MENU_REDRAW_STATUS);
2086  }
2087 
2088  if (pager_menu->redraw & MENU_REDRAW_FLOW)
2089  {
2090  if (!(priv->pview->flags & MUTT_PAGER_RETWINCH))
2091  {
2092  priv->lines = -1;
2093  for (int i = 0; i <= priv->topline; i++)
2094  if (!priv->line_info[i].continuation)
2095  priv->lines++;
2096  for (int i = 0; i < priv->max_line; i++)
2097  {
2098  priv->line_info[i].offset = 0;
2099  priv->line_info[i].type = -1;
2100  priv->line_info[i].continuation = 0;
2101  priv->line_info[i].chunks = 0;
2102  priv->line_info[i].search_cnt = -1;
2103  priv->line_info[i].quote = NULL;
2104 
2105  mutt_mem_realloc(&(priv->line_info[i].syntax), sizeof(struct TextSyntax));
2106  if (priv->search_compiled && priv->line_info[i].search)
2107  FREE(&(priv->line_info[i].search));
2108  }
2109 
2110  priv->last_line = 0;
2111  priv->topline = 0;
2112  }
2113  int i = -1;
2114  int j = -1;
2115  while (display_line(priv->fp, &priv->last_pos, &priv->line_info, ++i,
2116  &priv->last_line, &priv->max_line,
2117  priv->has_types | priv->search_flag | (priv->pview->flags & MUTT_PAGER_NOWRAP),
2118  &priv->quote_list, &priv->q_level, &priv->force_redraw,
2119  &priv->search_re, priv->pview->win_pager) == 0)
2120  {
2121  if (!priv->line_info[i].continuation && (++j == priv->lines))
2122  {
2123  priv->topline = i;
2124  if (!priv->search_flag)
2125  break;
2126  }
2127  }
2128  }
2129 
2130  if ((pager_menu->redraw & MENU_REDRAW_BODY) || (priv->topline != priv->oldtopline))
2131  {
2132  do
2133  {
2134  mutt_window_move(priv->pview->win_pager, 0, 0);
2135  priv->curline = priv->topline;
2136  priv->oldtopline = priv->topline;
2137  priv->lines = 0;
2138  priv->force_redraw = false;
2139 
2140  while ((priv->lines < priv->pview->win_pager->state.rows) &&
2141  (priv->line_info[priv->curline].offset <= priv->sb.st_size - 1))
2142  {
2143  if (display_line(priv->fp, &priv->last_pos, &priv->line_info,
2144  priv->curline, &priv->last_line, &priv->max_line,
2145  (priv->pview->flags & MUTT_DISPLAYFLAGS) | priv->hide_quoted |
2146  priv->search_flag | (priv->pview->flags & MUTT_PAGER_NOWRAP),
2147  &priv->quote_list, &priv->q_level, &priv->force_redraw,
2148  &priv->search_re, priv->pview->win_pager) > 0)
2149  {
2150  priv->lines++;
2151  }
2152  priv->curline++;
2153  mutt_window_move(priv->pview->win_pager, 0, priv->lines);
2154  }
2155  priv->last_offset = priv->line_info[priv->curline].offset;
2156  } while (priv->force_redraw);
2157 
2158  mutt_curses_set_color(MT_COLOR_TILDE);
2159  while (priv->lines < priv->pview->win_pager->state.rows)
2160  {
2161  mutt_window_clrtoeol(priv->pview->win_pager);
2162  if (c_tilde)
2163  mutt_window_addch(priv->pview->win_pager, '~');
2164  priv->lines++;
2165  mutt_window_move(priv->pview->win_pager, 0, priv->lines);
2166  }
2167  mutt_curses_set_color(MT_COLOR_NORMAL);
2168 
2169  /* We are going to update the pager status bar, so it isn't
2170  * necessary to reset to normal color now. */
2171 
2172  menu_queue_redraw(pager_menu, MENU_REDRAW_STATUS); /* need to update the % seen */
2173  }
2174 
2175  if (pager_menu->redraw & MENU_REDRAW_STATUS)
2176  {
2177  char pager_progress_str[65]; /* Lots of space for translations */
2178 
2179  if (priv->last_pos < priv->sb.st_size - 1)
2180  {
2181  snprintf(pager_progress_str, sizeof(pager_progress_str), OFF_T_FMT "%%",
2182  (100 * priv->last_offset / priv->sb.st_size));
2183  }
2184  else
2185  {
2186  const char *msg = (priv->topline == 0) ?
2187  /* L10N: Status bar message: the entire email is visible in the pager */
2188  _("all") :
2189  /* L10N: Status bar message: the end of the email is visible in the pager */
2190  _("end");
2191  mutt_str_copy(pager_progress_str, msg, sizeof(pager_progress_str));
2192  }
2193 
2194  /* print out the pager status bar */
2195  mutt_window_move(priv->pview->win_pbar, 0, 0);
2196  mutt_curses_set_color(MT_COLOR_STATUS);
2197 
2198  if (priv->pview->mode == PAGER_MODE_EMAIL || priv->pview->mode == PAGER_MODE_ATTACH_E)
2199  {
2200  const size_t l1 = priv->pview->win_pbar->state.cols * MB_LEN_MAX;
2201  const size_t l2 = sizeof(buf);
2202  const size_t buflen = (l1 < l2) ? l1 : l2;
2203  struct Email *e = (priv->pview->mode == PAGER_MODE_EMAIL) ?
2204  priv->pview->pdata->email : // PAGER_MODE_EMAIL
2205  priv->pview->pdata->body->email; // PAGER_MODE_ATTACH_E
2206 
2207  const char *const c_pager_format =
2208  cs_subset_string(NeoMutt->sub, "pager_format");
2209  mutt_make_string(buf, buflen, priv->pview->win_pbar->state.cols,
2210  NONULL(c_pager_format), m, priv->pview->pdata->ctx->msg_in_pager,
2211  e, MUTT_FORMAT_NO_FLAGS, pager_progress_str);
2212  mutt_draw_statusline(priv->pview->win_pbar,
2213  priv->pview->win_pbar->state.cols, buf, l2);
2214  }
2215  else
2216  {
2217  char bn[256];
2218  snprintf(bn, sizeof(bn), "%s (%s)", priv->pview->banner, pager_progress_str);
2219  mutt_draw_statusline(priv->pview->win_pbar,
2220  priv->pview->win_pbar->state.cols, bn, sizeof(bn));
2221  }
2222  mutt_curses_set_color(MT_COLOR_NORMAL);
2223  const bool c_ts_enabled = cs_subset_bool(NeoMutt->sub, "ts_enabled");
2224  if (c_ts_enabled && TsSupported && priv->menu)
2225  {
2226  const char *const c_ts_status_format =
2227  cs_subset_string(NeoMutt->sub, "ts_status_format");
2228  menu_status_line(buf, sizeof(buf), shared, priv->menu, sizeof(buf),
2229  NONULL(c_ts_status_format));
2230  mutt_ts_status(buf);
2231  const char *const c_ts_icon_format =
2232  cs_subset_string(NeoMutt->sub, "ts_icon_format");
2233  menu_status_line(buf, sizeof(buf), shared, priv->menu, sizeof(buf),
2234  NONULL(c_ts_icon_format));
2235  mutt_ts_icon(buf);
2236  }
2237  }
2238 
2239  pager_menu->redraw = MENU_REDRAW_NO_FLAGS;
2240  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG5, "repaint done\n");
2241 }
